response.justification: The candidate is a strong technical match on paper — deep TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience, LLM/MCP and AI-agent tooling work, Kubernetes and AWS exposure all align with the required and nice-to-have skills. However, he is a Staff-level engineer with roughly 15+ years of experience applying to an explicitly entry-level junior/mid role, which raises serious questions about salary alignment, retention, and role fit. His frontend depth is more Ember.js than React, PostgreSQL is not explicitly evidenced, and only basic German plus a high on-site requirement add friction for a German renewable-energy platform. Worth a conversation only if the seniority and compensation mismatch can be resolved upfront.
strengths: Extensive TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience directly matching the core stack
Hands-on LLM/MCP, AI-agent tooling, Kubernetes, and AWS experience covering nearly all nice-to-haves
Proven track record of testable, craftsmanship-driven development (TDD, CI/CD) and knowledge sharing
concerns: Significant overqualification for an entry-level role, with retention and salary-fit risk
React experience is thin relative to Ember.js, and PostgreSQL is not explicitly demonstrated
Only basic German and unclear location consistency versus a high on-site presence requirement

key_factors.factor: TypeScript/Node fullstack and AI/cloud skill alignment
